theory.intro: Unit fractions all have a numerator of $1$. The **larger the denominator, the smaller the fraction**, so they can be ordered just by their denominators.
facts: On a number line from $0$ to $1$, $\tfrac{1}{n}$ sits $\tfrac{1}{n}$ of the way along.
To order, compare the **denominators** — the largest denominator gives the smallest fraction.
steps: Order the denominators (smallest denominator = largest fraction).
Write the fractions in the matching order.
